title: [GPIO Interface] Verify XX_MEMTRIP_N output pin assertion
description:
(Sections with * are mandatory. You must fill them in, or say "Not Applicable" or "N/A" if you have seriously considered it and determined there's nothing to say. Remove these instructions and the explanatory text in each section as you fill things in.)


Flow *

XX_MEMTRIP_N is single output pin at the package level. It is used to communicate critical temperature of memory devices.


    Configuration *

    How does the platform need to be configured to obtain the desired coverage? For example: fuses, bios knobs, pre-test scripts, kernel/OS config. In the case of BIOS knobs, list all that impact this coverage, including both custom and default.


    Pass/Fail criteria *

    Examples: register values to look for, what content output to look for on the screen, key words or sections in the log.


    Intent Verification Strategy *

    How will TC logic be checked out prior to testing on silicon (e.g. on N-1 hardware, in Simics, etc.)? If not possible, explain why (e.g. new feature not present in N-1 and not modelled in pre-si environment).


    HW Requirements

    What besides silicon is required for execution of this test? For example: logic analyzer, a thermal head, an Intel test card or IHV device, etc.


    SW Requirements

    What software besides actual test content is needed for execution of this test? For example: special version of operating system, drivers, firmware, or harassers (to run with the main test content), software tool(s).


    Coverage

    Specify any expected coverage that is dependent on environment or configuration (not covered in the TCD's description). Also specify any coverage assessment/measurement technique that might be used, if applicable and if not stated in the TCD.


    Component Interactions

    Describe or illustrate the components that are involved and how they interact. This section is only needed if it provides more information than the TCD article, perhaps specific to the validation environment and framework used for this test_case.
